1.text: Greetings, stranger. You startled me, but that's nothing new around here...

4.text: Come, sit with me for a moment, $PLAYER$.

5.text:
Wealth comes in many forms. Food, health and a good home. That's true wealth.

However, a few years ago, things started to change...

6.text:
Some say we had it too good for too long. Others say the gods turned their backs on us...

<Vincent hesitates - his eyes seem lost, troubled by something.>

8.text:
To think that Lord Revere, the god of Hope and Prosperity, has left us... blasphemous.

<Vincent gazes at the campfire, perhaps talking to himself more than you.>

No, there is something else going on, but I do not wish to put myself inbetween the Council and the Church of St. Revere.

9.text:
That's exactly correct, $PLAYER$. I have a good feeling about you, my $G=boy:girl$. I can tell instantly about people, it's a gift.

What do you say?

11.text:
<$NPC$ appears to be sizing you up.>

Say now, $PLAYER$...
Are you lookin' for some quick gold? 

No tricks, on my honor.

20.text:
Yeah? Who are you and what do you want?

<Emitt looks right through you, clearly unimpressed>

If you're another Fechmar messenger then you better tuck tail and get out of here before you get on my nerves.

25.text:
What? I've got nothin' else to say to ya, pal.

Head down the road here, the priests' house isn't far.

75.text:
Yeah, what is it?

<Emitt sighs impatiently>

Zedek doesn't pay me enough for this.

89.text:
I really hope Crescent Lake becomes safe again soon...

<$NPC$ sighs sadly>

Where else am I going to gather my beautiful Cheryb Flowers?

91.text:
Ah, $PLAYER$. How are ye?

<Darius shows you the new harness hes made>

See? Nice, eh?

Thanks again for helping out.

93.text: What can I do for ya, $class$?

109.text: Move along now, St. Revere isn't far, just follow the road and the guard outposts will lead the way.

112.text: Move along now, St. Revere isn't far, just follow the road and the guard outposts will lead the way.

117.text: Move along now, St. Revere isn't far, just follow the road and the guard outposts will lead the way.

121.text: Move along now, St. Revere isn't far, just follow the road and the guard outposts will lead the way.

123.text:
The Lord watches over us, friend, even when the road seems darkest.

Some say it's a test of our faith.

124.text: Welp, unfortunately the meeting really didn't shed any light on anything... I guess we'll have to continue our investigation while Leon keeps the council happy. I'll keep an eye on Azralus in the meantime...

127.text:
<Thaddeus' eyebrows are gone and the hair in the front of his head is missing.>

Its always good to try new experiments to come up with new and useful products, even if sometimes they blow up in your face.

<Thaddeus smiles.>

128.text:
<You see Thaddeus poking a needle at some sort of bloody object.>

Greetings, good master. I am Thaddeus, the apothecary.

129.text: <Arjay pays no attention to you as he mumbles inaudibly and writes in his log book.>

130.text: Hello. I am Arjay, the lead Inspector for the town.

131.text: I just know my family will love the new house, $PLAYER$. I can't wait to get started!

132.text: Good day, my friend, I'm Lawrence, the local builder. You know the Church of Revere? My great great great grandfather built that with his brother, hundreds of years ago.

133.text:
<Briggs sighs.>

With all this recent trouble, its making this job much more stressful. I used to be able to just perch here on the wall and read all day, now there's an interruption every few minutes...

I knew I should've taken that cushy sargeant job when I had the chance... but it wasn't even any extra pay....

<Briggs mumbles off.>

134.text:
<Briggs is reading his book.>

If you're ever looking for a good book, the library on the other side of town is stocked. The librarian's name is Tessa, nice gal, quite the looker too...

<Briggs winks.>

135.text:
I wish I could tell you more, but for now it's the hurry up and wait game. Bureaucracy and politics can be a dangerous game, and I'm not a very good player.

I will let you know when something happens. I'll keep an eye on Azralus in the meantime...

136.text:
Always stay alert, you never know what can happen... especially now.

<Lamuel gives you a nod of respect.>

137.text:
<Regal grips his keychain on his belt.>

I'm going to try to only take duty inside the town from now on... I'm honestly too afraid to step outside the walls anymore...

138.text: Be careful when travelling outside of town... Its not like it used to be.

143.text:
Hey there, you look thirsty. 

Do you want to try some freshly brewed beer?

145.text:
I'll always have a good business here. 

People are always willing to pay for a quick fix to a complex problem. 

I had a gentleman come in last week looking for a potion that would keep him young forever... I told him to stop visiting drinking ten ales every day.

146.text: Didn't I already pay you? Sorry, but I'm busy concocting here and I can't be interrupted, else something might blow up!

147.text:
Run along now, little one.

I may be blind but I'm not dumb. That Vincent... found another prodigy, has he?

<$NPC$ scoffs>

You're weak, $PLAYER$. I can sense that much. Come back some other time.

151.text: Remember, don't wash your mushrooms until you're ready to use them, else they get mushy and inedible.

152.text: Good day, my friend! Isn't this a beautiful day? Perfect for just about any adventure!

154.text:
Hello, stranger! Are you hungry? My name is Jacques and I'm the best chef on this island.

Don't believe me? Just ask around, but not Zedek.

155.text:
Zedek asked me to be his head chef, but I told him I wasn't interested, so he doesn't like me too much.
I don't think very much of him either, his father was a much better man and actually did a lot of good for this town.I'm bot sure waht happened to him, but I do remember as a child he always seemed like a good kid.Now all he cares about is money and power.<Jacques shrugs.>

158.text:
Howdy, stranger! Hows it going? Silveroak Park is the right place for some peace and quiet. I work to make sure everything is neat and clean.

See those statues over there?

<Kent turns and points at the statues>

They honor the founding fathers of St. Revere.

160.text:
Good day, stranger, I think I've heard about you. Your name is $PLAYER$, right? I'm Vivien.

<You watch Vivien as she carefully assembles a book, being very impressed by her skilful work>

As you can see, I make books. I just love the smell and sound of flipping through the pages a new book, theres nothing else like it.

161.text: May Zorn guide you on your journey, $PLAYER$. If you wish to become a follower of Zorn, we can make that happen.

164.text: Greetings, Stranger. What brings you here? Not a lot of people come to this place. This is the Altar of Zorn and I am here to serve Zorn, the God of the earth and elements.

165.text:
<Jamil stands up tall as you approach.>

Welcome to our little saltwater fishing community. I hope you're not a bandit.

166.text: We live here not only because it’s close to the ocean, but also because we don't wish to serve Revere. We are welcome to go there to Saint Revere to trade or sell our fish, but other than that we like to keep to ourselves.

168.text: Hi I'm Garett, and I'm a professional angler. I've been fishing all my life, and I love it.

173.text: Good day, friend. You look well-traveled, so I’m guessing you’re not one of those idiots from St. Revere. My name is Tybalt. Lived on this island my whole life.

175.text:
<Julya is fixing her chef's apron.>

Hello, I am Julya, an apprentice to Jacques. I do all his prep work and manage the kitchen. I hope one day I can be as skilled as he is.

177.text:
Hi I'm Nina. Nice to see new faces around here.

I can't say that it happens very often.

182.text:
Things are really looking grim around here... 

To tell the you the truth, if I didn't have a young son, I'd be out of this place.

184.text:
Well now... a $class$.

<The scar faced man gazes at you>

You're in the wrong neck of the woods.

$NPC$'s the name. I'm a big-game hunter, plain as. 
That's Piotr... little bugger's been following me since St. Revere, but he's harmless enough.

Just set up camp here this morning.
Well then, $PLAYER$... what can I do for you?

187.text:
Howdy, stranger.

<The goblin's voice is shockingly deep with an accent not from around here>

You alright there, big fella'?
What, you ain't never seen a goblin before or somethin'?

Hey, listen pal - I follow the money. You might be thinking, "now how are you gonna find coin way out here"... and yet, I see a transaction in my future. Imagine that.

191.text: Hi, I'm Herdith. I'm looking for wild mushrooms. They can be quite tasty and can be useful in a lot of different medicine. You need to be careful though, because the wrong mushroom can make you very ill or even send you to the grave! If you have questions about which mushroom is okay to eat, just let me know!

197.text: Good day, friend. You look like you’ve been getting about quite a bit. Welcome to our little community. We don’t get a lot of visits from strangers here. My name is Jordy and you could say I'm a healer.

200.text: Wow! A new face! Did you get lost and end up here like I did? I barely escaped from those bastard zombies... unbelievable, but now I'm stuck here...

202.text: Hey there, how ye doin? You got any news for us? Is the world out there fallin' apart or what?

203.text:
<Samel becomes wide-eyed.>

Oh geez... you really think so? Well... I'm not sure, but if you can make it all the way here, we should probably group and flee before it's too late... but again... uhhh.. I dunno maybe we should here instead... it looks pretty rough out there.

204.text:
Yes.. you're right. We should stay here and ride it out.

Maybe that Azralus guy was right and I should've attended church as a kid...

207.text: Hail traveler, good to see you.

212.text:
My name is Frederic... Doctor Frederic. 

My passion is natural medicine... flora and fauna... there are many strange and wonderful things that occur in nature, but nobody takes the time to really look and study.

215.text: <Miriam is mumbling to herself... something about a cat.>

216.text:
<You notice Yoan's face looks a bit jaundiced.>

Ohh... I just can't seem to shake this fever and chills...

Jordy is trying to come up with a solution. My wife is sicker than I am and in bed.

219.text: Please, don't be afraid. I am Dante and I used to be a priest of the Church of Revere and an elder of the town. As you can see, my physical life is over, but my soul refuses to rest.

220.text: I was out here a few weeks ago visting the cemetery, when a foul zombie jumped out of the bushes and stabbed me with some wretched weapon. He quickly ran off, but I recognized him as Goresh, a defier of the Church of Revere and enemy of the townsfolk. Goresh and his followers were executed several years ago by the council of Revere, which I oversaw at the time. As a result of the attack, I am stuck here, seemingly forever.

221.text: <You take a big sip of beer from a mug she hands you and rub your belly in satisfaction>

223.text:
Hi, I'm Grover. My time is spent running around this town delivering messages. 

It's good pay, but I get tired. I eat muffins all day long to give me energy. Alaria is quite the baker, you should pay her a visit!

227.text: Begone peasant, before I call the guards.

229.text: You ever seen an exploding Antling? Well I have! I made two last week in my basement!

234.text:
What's this? A mortal being? What do you think you're doing here $G=boy:girl$? Perhaps... you are suicidal, or perhaps you seek me out. I guess we will find out.

<Mastemia smiles at you.>

236.text: Hey there feller, welcome to my farm. I'm a generous guy but don't be stealing anything!

238.text: Through this entrance is the Eversorrow Forest. You should stay out unless you know what you're doing.

240.text:
My, my, my... '$PLAYER$', was it?

<$NPC$ is playing with his beard as he eyes you with an eccentric smile>

I think that we will become most good friends.
Most good friends, indeed.

245.text:
I honestly haven't a clue. Well, just that...

<$NPC$ thinks for a moment>

I overheard a drunkard at the Inn of St. Revere blathering about the undead. Nothing new there, but he insisted they're cunning deceivers. How did he put it... "they're tricky snakes playing us for fools!", he shouted. 

He wasn't alone, but I didn't linger.

246.text:
The man claimed, ah - how did he put it...

<$NPC$ plays with his beard as he quotes the drunkard>

"I was mindin' me own business. That's when I seen 'em beyond the trees. Watching the guards. Tallest undead I ever  seen. Looked me in the eyes, he did. When the guard called my name... it just up'n left, calm and cold as ice!"

He said it happened on the road near the Ruins of At'mar.

247.text:
How the hell should I know?!

<$NPC$'s tone is quiet but stern>

The undead attack us. 
What difference does it make if they're deft or dull?

I'll speak no more of it. I've told you all I know.

248.text:
Did they now? 

Well I very seriously doubt that it had anything to do with the flower. It's a very common and plain flower. 

A run of bad luck is all, but nothing you couldn't handle.
The right $G=man:woman$ for the job, indeed!

<$NPC$'s attempts to sound cheery are labored at best>

250.text:
Prefer? I'm left handed, but what do you mean?

<$NPC$ seems notably concerned>

What are you getting at?

251.text:
N-Now... there's no need for that.

<$NPC$ is notably sweating>

Alright, fine. The peddler that's buying these artifacts... he says that the undead are attracted to them.

Total nonsense I told myself!
But, well... I had to be sure, right?

252.text:
Yes... and that's all there is to it.
Honestly, I thought it was nonsense and that no harm would come to you.

Well then, satisfied?

253.text:
I'm glad that we had this little chat.

Bye now, $class$.

254.text:
<$NPC$'s fright is obvious but he remains defiant>

Fie! I'm not lying. Listen, I didn't know for sure it would work. I had... a man I trust told me it was true, that he saw it himself. I just wanted to know what I was dealing with. 

These artifacts are fetching a high price. Here, you can have them. I owe you that much. Fair?

255.text:
He calls himself Billings.
If you have a map then I will show you.

<$NPC$ is relieved but clearly disappointed>

256.text:
Why hello fine traveler, how do you fare on such a lovely day?

<The goblin sounds like an eccentric middle aged man but looks and speaks with the accent of an elder>

Billings' the name, $G=my good sir:my lady$. A pleasure to meet you.
A young $G=lad:lass$ like yourself must be looking for fortune!

Hm? Is something on your mind, then?

258.text:
Ah, back again are you? I see, I see. 
I've become quite popular, wouldn't you agree?

<$NPC$ chuckles faintly under his breath>

260.text:
Ah, heading for St. Revere? Well met, noble $CLASS$. 

I'm not surprised to see your kind here. Heard rumor of fortune, did you? Wouldn't mind some fortune, myself.

Just follow the road directly south from here.

I wouldn't recommend the road to the east. It'll get you there same, but I've heard unfriendly rumors about that route.

262.text:
Ah, if it isn't my favorite $class$.
Well then, $PLAYER$ - what business have you?

263.text:
Good day to you, $G=sir:madam$.

Nice day, isn't it?

264.text: Why, yes am I am. How could you tell?

265.text:
Certainly. 

And you strike me as a... mercenary?
Why no, you're a $CLASS$.

Or perhaps both?

In any case, you'll fair better than myself, I'm sure.

266.text:
Hm? Oh, nothing... my mind wanders. 
It's just that, well - I must say. 

This is no island for old men.

<$NPC$ seems weary from travel>

Well then, will there be anything else?

267.text:
You're in luck my dear $G=boy:girl$. 
I have much coin and you have much need.

Unanswered questions...
What a nuisance that would be.

271.text: Well, what can I do for ye?

272.text:
Swords and Daggers, that's my trade.

See anything you like, $CLASS$?

274.text:
Aye, what'll it be, $CLASS$.

See anything ye like, $G=lad:lass$?

275.text:
Hi there! $PLAYER$, was it?
Nice to meet you. 

Feel free to take a look around.

278.text:
Hmm... a $CLASS$?

What business have you?

279.text:
Greetings, stranger. Name's $NPC$, a sword for hire. I'm not surprised to see your kind here. Many $class$'s have traveled this way since the recent troubles began.

Do you need any help?

280.text: What do you need directions to?

282.text:
They're dead, they don't care...

<$NPC$ sighs in fatigue>

It didn't use to be this way. 
I feel like we're losing our way as a people.

284.text:
The name's $NPC$, I'm a butcher. 
It's good to see a friendly face.

Well, what'll it be?

285.text: <$NPC$ is preoccupied and pays you no attention>

286.text:
Swords, axes, shields, armor...
All in high demand these days.

The market of St. Revere use to be very different.

<$NPC$ sighs>

A sign of the times, I suppose.

287.text:
$NPC$'s the name, $class$. I'm a businessman, plain as. An entrepreneur of sorts. My caravan brings goods into St. Revere from the west.

<$NPC$ is notably tired>

I've made a fortune lately, but the demand is tough to keep up with. Hard to believe that of all places, St. Revere was sitting on so much coin.

288.text:
Well, it's just... don't you find it strange?

St. Revere for years has been the envy of this island we call Dreadmyst... never knew how you guys did it. 'Faith! Praise the Lords!', or so it goes. 

A peaceful place like this suddenly buying up every sword and shield in sight? Hiring every mercenary that walks into town? 

What changed, and where's the gold come from?

289.text:
Beats me. But, money talks.

<$NPC$ pauses to think>

I couldn't say for sure exactly how many other caravans come and go... but I wouldn't be surprised if these folk have bought enough armaments to raise an army.

<$NPC$ shrugs>

290.text: Students gifted with a keen intellect and unwavering discipline may walk the path of the mage. The arcane magic available to magi is both great and dangerous, and thus is revealed only to the most devoted practitioners.

291.text:
Clerics are devoted to the spiritual, and we express our unwavering faith by serving the people. 

For millennia we have left behind the confines of the church to support our allies in war-torn lands. In the midst of terrible conflict, no hero questions the value of our priestly order.

293.text:
Now see here, $class$ - if you want the latest news than simply tend to the Town Cryer downtown.

Run along now.

295.text:
<$NPC$ appears caught off guard>

That Vincent... let me tell you something, little $G=boy:girl$. That is none of your concern.

296.text:
Well certainly, we owe a great deal to sir Zedek.

Without him, we'd be lost. 
A wealthy philanthropist such as himself is a gift from the Gods, no doubt.

298.text:
My name is $NPC$ and as you can see,

<He gestures at the house>

This is my home.

302.text:
<$NPC$ rubs his hands together>

Ah, another fine $CLASS$...
How may I be of service?

303.text: Queue solo or with a partner for 2v2 arena battles. Matchmaking pairs fighters of similar rating. Victories increase your rating while defeats lower it. Beating stronger opponents earns more points. Losing to weaker ones costs more. Climb the ranks and prove yourself.

304.text:
No coin, no barter. Put your gold away, traveler. What kind of servant would I be if I charged for what was given to me by grace? The Lord provides, I'm just the vessel.

Now, what can I reshape for you?

305.text:
You're probably wondering why I'm here.
So am I, frankly.

Well then, what will it be?
